title: NullPointerException thrown
fixes: Add null check before accessing the object
Use Optional<T> to wrap potentially null values
Initialize the variable at declaration time
Use @NonNull annotation to catch at compile time

title: Class not found at runtime
fixes: Verify the class is on the classpath
Check for typos in the fully qualified class name
Add the missing dependency to pom.xml or build.gradle

title: Method not found at runtime
fixes: Check for version mismatch between compile-time and runtime dependencies
Run mvn dependency:tree to find conflicting versions
Exclude the transitive dependency causing the conflict

title: Java heap space exhausted
fixes: Increase heap size with -Xmx flag (e.g., -Xmx2g)
Profile memory usage with VisualVM or Eclipse MAT to find leaks
Check for unbounded collections or caches growing without eviction
Use streaming/pagination instead of loading all data into memory

title: Collection modified during iteration
fixes: Use Iterator.remove() instead of Collection.remove() during iteration
Use ConcurrentHashMap or CopyOnWriteArrayList for concurrent access
Create a copy of the collection before iterating

title: Stack overflow from deep recursion
fixes: Add a base case to terminate recursion
Convert recursive algorithm to iterative with an explicit stack
Increase stack size with -Xss flag if recursion depth is legitimate

title: Array index out of bounds
fixes: Check array length before accessing index
Use a for-each loop instead of index-based access
Verify loop boundary conditions (use < array.length, not <=)

title: Invalid class cast
fixes: Use instanceof check before casting
Review generic type parameters for type erasure issues
Use pattern matching (Java 16+) instead of explicit casts

title: Network IO connection failure
fixes: Verify the target service is running and accessible
Check firewall rules and network connectivity
Add retry logic with exponential backoff
Increase connection timeout settings

title: Spring Boot bean not found for injection
fixes: Add @Component, @Service, or @Repository annotation to the class
Verify component scanning includes the package (@ComponentScan)
Check that the bean's conditional annotations are satisfied
Use @Primary or @Qualifier to resolve ambiguity

regex: Consider defining a bean of type.*in your configuration
title: Spring bean not defined in configuration
fixes: Create a @Bean method in a @Configuration class
Add @EnableAutoConfiguration or the specific @Enable annotation
Check that the auto-configuration dependency is on the classpath

regex: Could not resolve dependencies for project
title: Maven dependency resolution failed
fixes: Run mvn dependency:resolve to see detailed errors
Check if the repository URL is accessible
Verify the artifact coordinates (groupId, artifactId, version) are correct
Clear local repository cache: rm -rf ~/.m2/repository/<group>

title: Gradle Java compilation failed
fixes: Check the compiler error messages above this line for the actual issue
Verify sourceCompatibility matches your JDK version
Run ./gradlew compileJava --info for detailed output

title: Metaspace memory exhausted
fixes: Increase metaspace with -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=512m
Check for classloader leaks (common in hot-reloading scenarios)
Reduce the number of loaded classes or use a single classloader

title: Spring circular dependency detected
fixes: Refactor to remove the circular dependency (extract shared logic to a third bean)
Use @Lazy on one of the injection points to break the cycle
Switch from constructor injection to setter injection for one dependency

title: File not found on filesystem
fixes: Verify the file path is correct and the file exists
Use getClass().getResourceAsStream() for classpath resources
Check file permissions and working directory

title: Class compiled with newer JDK than runtime
fixes: Upgrade your runtime JDK to match or exceed the compile version
Set -source and -target flags to compile for the older JDK
Set sourceCompatibility in Gradle to the target runtime version

title: Spring unsatisfied dependency injection
fixes: Check that the required bean exists and is properly annotated
Use @Autowired(required = false) if the dependency is optional
Verify there is no typo in @Qualifier value

title: Duplicate key in stream Collectors.toMap
fixes: Provide a merge function as third argument to Collectors.toMap()
Use Collectors.groupingBy() instead if duplicates are expected
Filter duplicates upstream before collecting

regex: Plugin.*was not found in any of the following sources
title: Gradle plugin not found
fixes: Add the plugin's repository to settings.gradle pluginManagement block
Check that the plugin ID and version are correct
Verify internet connectivity or configure offline repository

regex: cannot borrow.*as mutable because it is also borrowed as immutable
title: Mutable borrow while immutable borrow exists
fixes: Clone the data to avoid shared references
Restructure code so immutable borrow ends before mutable borrow begins
Use RefCell<T> for interior mutability if runtime checking is acceptable

title: Value does not live long enough for lifetime
fixes: Extend the lifetime of the borrowed value by moving it to an outer scope
Clone the value instead of borrowing it
Use owned types (String instead of &str, Vec instead of &[T])

title: Required trait not implemented
fixes: Add #[derive(TraitName)] to the struct if it's a standard trait
Implement the trait manually with an impl block
Check if a different type that implements the trait should be used

title: Use of value after move
fixes: Clone the value before the move if it implements Clone
Use a reference (&T) instead of moving ownership
Restructure code to avoid needing the value after it's moved

title: Type mismatch in expression
fixes: Convert the type explicitly using .into() or as keyword
Check function signature for expected parameter types
Use From/Into trait implementations for clean conversion

title: Undefined value in scope
fixes: Check for typos in the variable name
Bring the value into scope with use statement
Verify the variable is declared before use in the current scope

title: Unused variable warning
fixes: Prefix the variable with underscore: _variable_name
Remove the variable if it's truly unnecessary
Use the variable or add #[allow(unused_variables)] if intentional

regex: cannot borrow.*as mutable more than once at a time
title: Multiple mutable borrows
fixes: Restructure to use only one mutable reference at a time
Split the struct into separate fields that can be borrowed independently
Use Cell<T> or RefCell<T> for interior mutability

title: Borrow of value after it was moved
fixes: Clone the value before the move
Use references instead of moving ownership
Restructure code so the borrow happens before the move

regex: the size for values of type.*cannot be known at compilation time
title: Unsized type used where sized is required
fixes: Box the value: Box<dyn Trait> instead of dyn Trait
Use a reference &dyn Trait instead of owned dyn Trait
Add Sized bound or use ?Sized where appropriate

title: Expected type but found unit ()
fixes: Remove the trailing semicolon from the last expression in the block
Add an explicit return statement with the correct value
Verify the function return type matches what's being returned

regex: no method named.*found for.*in the current scope
title: Method not found on type
fixes: Import the trait that provides the method with use statement
Check the type — you may need to dereference or convert first
Verify the method exists for this specific generic instantiation

regex: cannot move out of.*which is behind a shared reference
title: Cannot move out of shared reference
fixes: Clone the value instead of moving it
Use .as_ref() or pattern match with ref keyword
Change the function to take ownership instead of a reference

title: Missing lifetime annotation
fixes: Add explicit lifetime parameter: fn foo<'a>(x: &'a str) -> &'a str
Return an owned type instead of a reference if possible
Use 'static lifetime if the data lives for the entire program

title: Undeclared crate or module
fixes: Add the crate to [dependencies] in Cargo.toml
Run cargo build to download the dependency
Check crate name spelling (use hyphens in Cargo.toml, underscores in code)

regex: thread '.*' panicked at '.*index out of bounds
title: Array/vector index out of bounds panic
fixes: Use .get(index) which returns Option instead of panicking
Check length before indexing
Use iterators instead of manual indexing

title: Type does not implement Send for async/threading
fixes: Ensure all types held across await points implement Send
Use Arc<Mutex<T>> instead of Rc<RefCell<T>> for thread-safe sharing
Restructure to drop non-Send values before the await point

regex: error\[E0599\].*no function or associated item named.*found
title: Function or associated item not found
fixes: Check for typos in the function name
Import the correct module or use the full path
Verify the method exists on the specific type (not a trait implementation)

regex: package.*was not found in the pkg-config search path
title: System library not found by pkg-config during build
fixes: Install the system development package (e.g., apt install libssl-dev)
Set PKG_CONFIG_PATH to include the library's .pc file directory
Use vendored feature flag if available (e.g., openssl = { features = ["vendored"] })

title: Cannot move out of dereference
fixes: Use .clone() to get an owned copy
Match with ref keyword to borrow instead of move
Use std::mem::take() or std::mem::replace() to swap values out

regex: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
title: Nil pointer dereference
fixes: Add nil check before accessing the pointer
Initialize the struct/pointer before use
Return early if a function returns nil instead of a valid pointer

title: Undefined variable or function
fixes: Check for typos in the identifier name
Import the package that defines the symbol
Ensure the variable is declared in the current scope or file

title: Unused import
fixes: Remove the unused import
Use the blank identifier: _ "package" if import is for side effects
Use goimports tool to auto-manage imports

title: Multi-value return used as single value
fixes: Assign both return values: val, err := function()
Use blank identifier for unused value: val, _ := function()
Wrap in a helper if you only need one value repeatedly

title: Type mismatch in assignment or argument
fixes: Perform explicit type conversion: targetType(value)
Check if the value needs a pointer/dereference: &val or *ptr
Verify interface satisfaction — the type may need additional methods

title: Goroutine deadlock - all blocked
fixes: Ensure channels have a reader and writer (don't send to unbuffered channel with no receiver)
Use buffered channels if producer/consumer timing differs
Add a select with default or timeout to prevent indefinite blocking

title: Variable declared but not used
fixes: Use the variable in your code
Remove the declaration if it's unnecessary
Assign to blank identifier _ if value must be captured but not used

title: Cannot assign to struct field or expression
fixes: Use a pointer receiver to modify struct fields
Store the struct as a pointer in the map: map[key]*Struct
Assign the full struct back to the map key after modification

title: Interface type assertion failed
fixes: Use comma-ok pattern: val, ok := iface.(Type)
Check the concrete type with a type switch
Ensure the type implements all methods of the target interface

title: Concurrent map access without synchronization
fixes: Protect the map with sync.RWMutex
Use sync.Map for concurrent access patterns
Restrict map access to a single goroutine and communicate via channels

title: Range over non-iterable type
fixes: Ensure the variable is a slice, array, map, channel, or string
Check if you need to dereference a pointer to a slice: *slicePtr
Convert the type to a rangeable form

regex: missing return at end of function
title: Function missing return statement
fixes: Add a return statement at the end of the function
Ensure all code paths in if/else/switch return a value
Use named return values with a bare return if appropriate

title: Context deadline/timeout exceeded
fixes: Increase the timeout duration in context.WithTimeout()
Optimize the slow operation to complete within the deadline
Add proper context cancellation handling in the called function

title: Send on closed channel
fixes: Ensure the channel is only closed by the sender, not the receiver
Use a sync.Once to close the channel exactly once
Add a done signal or context to stop senders before closing

title: Package not found in go.mod
fixes: Run go get package@version to add the dependency
Run go mod tidy to resolve and clean dependencies
Check the import path for typos or version suffix (v2, v3)

title: Slice bounds out of range
fixes: Check slice length before slicing: if len(s) > i
Use min(i, len(s)) for the upper bound
Validate input indices before using them for slicing

title: Cannot take address of expression
fixes: Assign the value to a variable first, then take its address
Use a helper function that returns a pointer: func ptr(v T) *T { return &v }
Restructure to avoid needing a pointer to a temporary value

title: Data race detected by race detector
fixes: Protect shared state with sync.Mutex or sync.RWMutex
Use atomic operations for simple counters: atomic.AddInt64()
Redesign to pass data through channels instead of shared memory

title: File descriptor limit reached
fixes: Ensure all file/connection handles are closed with defer f.Close()
Increase OS ulimit: ulimit -n 65536
Use connection pooling to limit concurrent open connections

title: Package initialization cycle
fixes: Break the cycle by moving shared types to a separate package
Use interfaces to decouple packages
Restructure imports so dependencies flow in one direction

regex: unexpectedly found nil while (unwrapping|implicitly unwrapping) an Optional
title: Force unwrap of nil Optional value
fixes: Use optional binding: if let value = optional { ... }
Use guard let for early exit: guard let value = optional else { return }
Provide a default with nil coalescing: optional ?? defaultValue

regex: Fatal error: Unexpectedly found nil while unwrapping an Optional value
title: Fatal nil unwrap at runtime
fixes: Replace force unwrap (!) with optional chaining (?.) or if-let binding
Check IBOutlet connections — disconnected outlets are nil at runtime
Initialize the property before accessing it

title: UI update called from background thread
fixes: Wrap UI code in DispatchQueue.main.async { ... }
Use @MainActor attribute on functions that update UI (Swift 5.5+)
Move the UI update to the completion handler on the main queue

title: Codable decoding type mismatch
fixes: Check JSON key types match your model's property types
Use String(describing:) and manual decoding for flexible types
Add custom init(from decoder:) to handle type variations

title: Codable key not found in JSON
fixes: Make the property optional: var field: Type?
Provide a default in custom init(from:) using decodeIfPresent
Add CodingKeys enum to map JSON keys to property names

regex: Modifications to the layout engine must not be performed from a background thread
title: Auto Layout modified from background thread
fixes: Dispatch constraint changes to main queue
Use @MainActor for async functions that modify layout
Move all UI/layout code to viewDidLoad or main thread callbacks

title: Auto Layout constraint conflict
fixes: Lower the priority of one conflicting constraint
Remove the redundant constraint causing the conflict
Set translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false on programmatic views
Use constraint debugging: po view.constraintsAffectingLayout(for:)

title: Memory leak from retain cycle
fixes: Use [weak self] in closures that are stored by the object
Use [unowned self] if self is guaranteed to outlive the closure
Break the cycle by using a delegate pattern with weak reference

title: Bad memory access (dangling pointer or zombie)
fixes: Enable Zombie Objects in scheme diagnostics to identify the freed object
Check for use-after-free with unowned references — use weak instead
Enable Address Sanitizer to catch memory corruption

regex: Cannot convert value of type.*to expected argument type
title: Type conversion error in function argument
fixes: Add explicit type cast or conversion: String(intValue), Int(stringValue)
Check if you need to unwrap an Optional before passing
Use as! or as? for class hierarchy conversions

title: Corrupted or invalid JSON data
fixes: Validate JSON format with a linter before decoding
Print the raw data as string to inspect: String(data: data, encoding: .utf8)
Add do-catch around decode and log the DecodingError details

title: Swift concurrency data race warning
fixes: Mark the type as @Sendable or make it conform to Sendable
Use actors to isolate mutable state
Copy the value before sending across concurrency boundaries

title: Escaping closure captures mutating self in struct
fixes: Change the struct to a class if it needs reference semantics
Capture specific properties instead of self
Use a local copy of self inside the closure

regex: The compiler is unable to type-check this expression in reasonable time
title: Swift type checker timeout on complex expression
fixes: Break the expression into smaller intermediate variables with explicit types
Add explicit type annotations to closures
Split complex chained operations into separate statements

regex: this class is not key value coding-compliant for the key
title: KVC non-compliant key (storyboard outlet mismatch)
fixes: Check Interface Builder connections — remove stale outlet connections
Ensure @IBOutlet property names match the storyboard references
Clean build folder and reconnect the outlet

title: Ambiguous function or property reference
fixes: Add explicit type annotation to disambiguate
Use full module-qualified name: ModuleName.functionName
Cast the result to the expected type

regex: Protocol.*can only be used as a generic constraint.*Self or associated type
title: Protocol with Self/associated type used as concrete type
fixes: Use a generic constraint: func foo<T: Protocol>(val: T)
Use type erasure: AnyProtocol wrapper
Use opaque return type: some Protocol (Swift 5.1+)

title: Object deallocated immediately after creation
fixes: Store the instance in a property to maintain a strong reference
Assign to a variable instead of discarding: let manager = Manager()
Check that a delegate or parent retains the object

title: Symbol not found in current scope
fixes: Import the module that defines the symbol
Check target membership — the file may not be in the current target
Verify spelling and case sensitivity of the identifier

title: Main actor isolation violation
fixes: Mark the calling function with @MainActor
Use await MainActor.run { } to dispatch to main actor
Make the property nonisolated if it doesn't require main actor

title: Kotlin NullPointerException
fixes: Use safe call operator ?. instead of direct access
Add null check with let: value?.let { ... }
Use Elvis operator for default: value ?: defaultValue

title: lateinit property accessed before initialization
fixes: Initialize the property before accessing it
Use ::property.isInitialized check before access
Consider using lazy { } delegation instead of lateinit
Move initialization to onCreate/onViewCreated lifecycle method

title: ClassCastException in Kotlin
fixes: Use safe cast: value as? TargetType
Check type before cast: if (value is TargetType) { ... }
Review generic type erasure — use reified type parameters inline

regex: Unable to find explicit activity class.*have you declared this activity in.*AndroidManifest
title: Activity not declared in AndroidManifest
fixes: Add <activity android:name=".YourActivity"/> to AndroidManifest.xml
Verify the fully qualified class name matches the manifest entry
Ensure the activity class is not in a library without manifest merger

title: Gradle dependency resolution failed
fixes: Add google() and mavenCentral() to repositories block
Check the dependency coordinates for typos
Run ./gradlew --refresh-dependencies to clear cache
Verify network connectivity and proxy settings

title: Jetpack Compose infinite recomposition loop
fixes: Move state changes out of the composable body into LaunchedEffect or event handlers
Use remember { } to prevent recreating objects on every recomposition
Ensure derivedStateOf is used for computed state that triggers recomposition

regex: Cannot access.*before supertype constructor has been called
title: Accessing member before super constructor completes
fixes: Move the initialization to an init block after super constructor
Use lazy initialization for the property
Pass the value as a constructor parameter instead

regex: Only the original thread that created a view hierarchy can touch its views
title: UI update from wrong thread on Android
fixes: Use runOnUiThread { } or view.post { } to update UI from background
Use withContext(Dispatchers.Main) in coroutines
Use LiveData/StateFlow which automatically dispatches to main thread

title: ViewModel missing no-arg constructor
fixes: Create a ViewModelFactory to provide constructor arguments
Use Hilt @HiltViewModel + @Inject constructor for DI
Use by viewModels { factory } delegate with custom factory

title: Unresolved reference in Kotlin
fixes: Import the missing class or function
Check that the dependency providing it is in build.gradle
Verify the Kotlin/Android SDK version supports this API

title: Nullable type where non-null expected
fixes: Use !! operator if you're certain it's non-null (risky)
Use safe call with let: value?.let { nonNull -> ... }
Provide a default with Elvis: value ?: throw IllegalStateException()

regex: Suspension functions can be called only within coroutine body
title: Suspend function called outside coroutine
fixes: Launch a coroutine: lifecycleScope.launch { suspendFun() }
Use viewModelScope.launch in ViewModel
Make the calling function also suspend

title: Fragment operation when not attached to Activity
fixes: Check isAdded before accessing context or activity
Use viewLifecycleOwner.lifecycleScope for Fragment coroutines
Cancel pending operations in onDestroyView

title: Kotlin compiler type incompatibility
fixes: Add explicit type conversion: .toInt(), .toString(), etc.
Check generic type parameters for variance issues
Verify function return type matches the expected type

title: Layout XML inflation error
fixes: Check for missing custom view class in the XML layout
Verify all required attributes are present in the XML
Ensure theme/style referenced in layout exists
Check that custom view has constructor with (Context, AttributeSet)

title: KAPT annotation processing failed
fixes: Check error above for the actual annotation processor failure
Clean and rebuild: ./gradlew clean assembleDebug
Update kapt dependencies to match library versions
Add kapt.incremental.apt=true to gradle.properties

title: Resource leak detected by StrictMode
fixes: Use .use { } block for Closeable resources (auto-closes)
Close cursors, streams, and connections in finally block
Use try-with-resources pattern: resource.use { ... }

title: Invalid notification configuration
fixes: Ensure notification channel is created before posting (Android 8+)
Set a valid small icon with setSmallIcon()
Check that PendingIntent has FLAG_IMMUTABLE or FLAG_MUTABLE

title: Background service start not allowed (Android 12+)
fixes: Use setForeground() for WorkManager expedited work
Use ForegroundService with foregroundServiceType declared in manifest
Schedule work with WorkManager instead of direct service start
Use exact alarms with SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM permission

title: OOM from bitmap allocation
fixes: Use image loading library (Coil/Glide) with automatic downsampling
Set inSampleSize in BitmapFactory.Options to load smaller images
Recycle bitmaps when no longer needed
Use hardware bitmaps where possible

title: Method called on nil object
fixes: Use safe navigation operator: object&.method
Add nil check: if object.present? or unless object.nil?
Use dig for nested hash access: hash.dig(:key1, :key2)

title: Undefined method on object
fixes: Check the object's class with .class — it may not be what you expect
Verify the method exists: object.respond_to?(:method_name)
Include the correct module or require the right gem

title: ActiveRecord record not found
fixes: Use find_by instead of find — it returns nil instead of raising
Add rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound for graceful handling
Verify the ID exists before querying

title: ActiveRecord validation failure on save!
fixes: Use save instead of save! to get false instead of exception
Check model.errors.full_messages for specific validation failures
Fix the data to satisfy validations or adjust validation rules

title: Rails routing error - no matching route
fixes: Run rails routes to see all available routes
Add the missing route to config/routes.rb
Check HTTP verb — you may be using GET when POST is defined
Verify the route helper name matches: resource_path vs resources_path

title: Pending database migrations
fixes: Run rails db:migrate to apply pending migrations
Run rails db:migrate:status to check migration status
If in production, run RAILS_ENV=production rails db:migrate

title: Pending migration prevents app start
fixes: Run rails db:migrate
If migration is broken, fix the migration file or run rails db:rollback
In test env: rails db:test:prepare or rails db:schema:load

regex: Bundler could not find compatible versions for gem
title: Gem version conflict in Bundler
fixes: Run bundle update <gem_name> to resolve the specific gem
Check Gemfile for overly strict version pins
Use bundle viz or bundler-audit to visualize dependency tree
Try removing Gemfile.lock and running bundle install fresh

title: Required file or gem not found
fixes: Install the missing gem: gem install <name> or add to Gemfile
Check require statement spelling and path
Run bundle install if the gem is in Gemfile but not installed

title: Uninitialized constant reference
fixes: Check class/module naming matches file path (Rails autoloading)
Add require or require_relative for the file defining the constant
Verify the namespace: use ::ClassName for top-level constants

title: Wrong number of arguments passed to method
fixes: Check the method signature for required vs optional parameters
Use keyword arguments for clarity: def method(name:, age: nil)
Verify you're not passing a hash without ** splat operator

title: Database column does not exist
fixes: Create a migration to add the column: rails g migration AddColumnToTable
Run pending migrations: rails db:migrate
Check if you're referencing a renamed or removed column

title: Required parameter missing from request
fixes: Ensure the form/request sends the required param nested correctly
Check strong params: params.require(:model).permit(:field)
Verify parameter nesting matches what the controller expects

title: Gem version mismatch at runtime
fixes: Run bundle exec before the command to use Bundler's resolved versions
Update the conflicting gem: bundle update <gem>
Remove version lock from Gemfile if too restrictive

regex: ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique.*duplicate key value violates unique constraint
title: Unique constraint violation in database
fixes: Add uniqueness validation to model: validates :field, uniqueness: true
Use find_or_create_by instead of create
Handle the exception with rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
Add unique index in migration if not present

title: Implicit type conversion error
fixes: Explicitly convert: .to_s, .to_i, .to_f as needed
Use string interpolation which calls to_s automatically: "value: #{var}"
Check if the variable is nil when String was expected

title: Ruby syntax error
fixes: Check for missing end keyword matching if/def/do/class
Look for unclosed string literals or parentheses
Verify heredoc syntax and terminators

title: Database does not exist
fixes: Run rails db:create to create the database
Check database.yml for correct database name and credentials
Verify PostgreSQL/MySQL service is running

title: Port already in use
fixes: Kill the process using the port: lsof -i :3000 then kill PID
Use a different port: rails s -p 3001
Use spring stop if Spring is holding the port

title: Undefined variable in view template
fixes: Pass the variable from controller: @variable in action
Use local_assigns.key?(:var) for partials with optional locals
Check the render call passes required locals: render partial: 'x', locals: {var: val}

title: Undefined variable used
fixes: Initialize the variable before use
Check for typos in the variable name
Use isset() or null coalescing: $var ?? 'default'
Verify variable scope — variables inside functions aren't global

title: PHP class not found
fixes: Run composer dump-autoload to regenerate autoload files
Check namespace matches directory structure (PSR-4)
Verify use statement at top of file imports the class
Ensure the package is installed: composer require vendor/package

title: Database table does not exist
fixes: Run php artisan migrate to create the table
Check table name in migration matches what the model expects
Set protected $table = 'table_name' in model if non-standard name

title: Duplicate key constraint violation
fixes: Use firstOrCreate() or updateOrCreate() instead of create()
Add unique validation rule before saving
Handle exception: try/catch QueryException
Use INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE for bulk operations

regex: Undefined.*offset|Trying to access array offset on.*null
title: Array offset undefined or null access
fixes: Check array key exists: isset($arr['key']) or array_key_exists()
Use null coalescing: $arr['key'] ?? 'default'
Verify the data source returns expected array structure

title: Blade view template not found
fixes: Check the view path matches file location: resources/views/path/file.blade.php
Verify dot notation matches directory separators
Clear view cache: php artisan view:clear
Check file extension is .blade.php not just .php

title: CSRF token validation failed
fixes: Add @csrf directive inside your form
For AJAX: include X-CSRF-TOKEN header from meta tag
Check session driver is configured and working
Exclude the route in VerifyCsrfToken middleware if needed (API routes)

regex: Session store not set on request
title: Session not available in request
fixes: Ensure the route is within the 'web' middleware group
Check session driver configuration in .env and config/session.php
Verify storage/framework/sessions directory exists and is writable

title: Laravel container cannot resolve class
fixes: Check controller namespace in route file (Laravel 8+ requires full namespace)
Register the binding in a Service Provider
Run composer dump-autoload
Verify the class file exists at the expected path

title: PHP max execution time exceeded
fixes: Optimize the slow operation (add database indexes, reduce loops)
Increase max_execution_time in php.ini for legitimate long operations
Use queued jobs for heavy processing: dispatch(new ProcessJob)
Paginate data instead of processing all records at once

title: PHP memory limit exhausted
fixes: Use chunk() or cursor() for large Eloquent queries
Increase memory_limit in php.ini if the operation legitimately needs more
Free memory by unsetting large variables: unset($data)
Use lazy collections for processing large datasets

title: Call to undefined static method
fixes: Check the class actually has this static method
Verify you're not calling an instance method statically
Check if the method is in a trait that needs to be used

title: Database column not found
fixes: Create a migration to add the column: php artisan make:migration
Check for typos in column name
If using $fillable, ensure the column name matches the database
Run php artisan migrate if migration exists but hasn't run

regex: The (GET|POST|PUT|DELETE) method is not supported for (route|this route)
title: HTTP method not supported for route
fixes: Check your form method matches the route definition
Add @method('PUT') or @method('DELETE') for spoofed methods
Verify route file uses correct Route::get/post/put/delete

title: Accessing undefined property on class
fixes: Declare the property in the class definition
Check for typos in the property name
For Eloquent: verify the column exists or accessor is defined
Use property_exists() check before access

title: Class not found during dependency injection
fixes: Run composer dump-autoload to regenerate class map
Verify the class namespace and file path follow PSR-4
Check the service provider registers the binding correctly
Clear config cache: php artisan config:clear

title: File write permission denied
fixes: Fix permissions: chmod -R 775 storage bootstrap/cache
Set correct ownership: chown -R www-data:www-data storage
Check the directory exists and parent is writable
Verify SELinux/AppArmor isn't blocking writes

regex: Your requirements could not be resolved to an installable set of packages
title: Composer dependency resolution failed
fixes: Run composer update --with-all-dependencies to resolve together
Check for PHP version requirements in failed packages
Use composer why-not vendor/package version to diagnose
Try removing composer.lock and running composer install fresh

title: Database connection refused
fixes: Verify database service is running
Check .env DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_DATABASE, DB_USERNAME, DB_PASSWORD
For Docker: use container name as host, not localhost
Clear config cache: php artisan config:clear after .env changes

title: Required field validation failure
fixes: Ensure the form input name matches the validation rule key
Check that the field is included in the request (not empty/missing)
For file uploads: add enctype='multipart/form-data' to form
Use nullable rule if the field should be optional

title: N+1 query problem detected
fixes: Use eager loading: includes(:association) in Rails, with() in Laravel
Use JOIN to fetch related data in a single query
Use batch loading: IN clause with collected IDs
Add query monitoring gem/package to detect in development

title: Sequential scan in EXPLAIN plan (missing index)
fixes: Add an index on the filtered/joined column
Check if the table statistics are stale: ANALYZE table_name
Verify the WHERE clause isn't wrapping the column in a function
Consider a partial index if only a subset of rows match

title: Index exists but not being used
fixes: Check if a function wraps the indexed column (kills index usage)
Verify data types match — implicit casting prevents index use
Update table statistics: ANALYZE/UPDATE STATISTICS
Check if the optimizer estimates a full scan is cheaper (small table)

title: Ambiguous column reference in JOIN
fixes: Prefix column with table alias: t.column_name
Add explicit table aliases to all tables in the query
Use fully qualified names in SELECT when joining tables with same column names

regex: column.*must appear in the GROUP BY clause|not.*aggregate.*or.*GROUP BY
title: Non-aggregated column missing from GROUP BY
fixes: Add the column to GROUP BY clause
Wrap the column in an aggregate function: MAX(), MIN(), ANY_VALUE()
Remove the column from SELECT if it's not needed
Use a window function instead if per-row values are needed

title: Window function used in invalid clause
fixes: Wrap the query in a subquery/CTE and filter on the outer query
Use QUALIFY clause if your database supports it (BigQuery, Snowflake)
Move the window function to SELECT and filter in a surrounding query

title: CTE recursion limit exceeded
fixes: Add a proper termination condition to the recursive member
Set higher limit: OPTION (MAXRECURSION n) in SQL Server
Add a depth counter column and WHERE depth < max_depth
Check for cycles in the data that cause infinite recursion

title: Database deadlock between transactions
fixes: Access tables in a consistent order across transactions
Keep transactions short — minimize lock hold time
Use SELECT ... FOR UPDATE NOWAIT and retry on failure
Add retry logic with exponential backoff in application code

regex: subquery returns more than.*row|Scalar subquery produced more than one element
title: Scalar subquery returned multiple rows
fixes: Add LIMIT 1 to the subquery if only one row is expected
Use IN or EXISTS instead of = for multi-row subqueries
Add additional WHERE conditions to narrow to one row
Use an aggregate: (SELECT MAX(col) FROM ...)

title: Division by zero in SQL expression
fixes: Use NULLIF: col / NULLIF(divisor, 0)
Add CASE WHEN divisor = 0 THEN NULL ELSE col/divisor END
Filter out zero divisors in WHERE clause
Use COALESCE to provide a default: COALESCE(col / NULLIF(d, 0), 0)

title: String value exceeds column length
fixes: Increase column size: ALTER TABLE ALTER COLUMN col VARCHAR(longer)
Truncate input in application layer before insert
Use TEXT type instead of VARCHAR if length is unpredictable
Add validation to reject oversized inputs at the application level

title: ORDER BY column not in GROUP BY or aggregate
fixes: Add the ORDER BY column to GROUP BY
Wrap ORDER BY column in an aggregate function
Use a subquery: aggregate first, then sort the result

title: Query execution timeout
fixes: Add indexes on filtered and joined columns
Simplify the query — break into smaller steps with temp tables
Increase statement_timeout for legitimate long-running queries
Check EXPLAIN plan for full table scans or nested loops

regex: INSERT.*violates not-null constraint|cannot insert NULL into
title: NOT NULL constraint violation
fixes: Provide a value for the required column in the INSERT
Add a DEFAULT value to the column definition
Make the column nullable if NULL is a valid state
Set a default in the application layer before saving

title: Table or relation does not exist
fixes: Check the table name spelling and schema prefix
Run pending migrations to create the table
Verify you're connected to the correct database
Check schema search_path settings in PostgreSQL

title: LATERAL join not supported or misused
fixes: Use CROSS APPLY / OUTER APPLY in SQL Server instead
Rewrite as a correlated subquery in the SELECT clause
Use a regular JOIN with a derived table if LATERAL isn't available

title: Insufficient privileges on database object
fixes: GRANT SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE on table TO role
Check the current user: SELECT current_user
Verify schema permissions: GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA to role
Use the correct role/user in your connection string

regex: operator does not exist.*=.*|No operator matches the given name and argument type
title: Type mismatch in comparison operator
fixes: Cast one side to match: column::text = $1 or CAST(col AS type)
Check parameter types match column types in the query
Use appropriate comparison function for the data type

regex: duplicate key value violates unique constraint
title: Unique constraint violation on insert/update
fixes: Use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE (PostgreSQL upsert)
Use MERGE or INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE (MySQL)
Check for the existing record before inserting
Review if the unique constraint is correct for your data model

title: Expensive correlated subquery or nested loop
fixes: Rewrite correlated subquery as a JOIN
Use a CTE to materialize the subquery result once
Add an index on the correlated column to speed inner lookups
Use EXISTS instead of IN for existence checks with large sets

title: Pod in CrashLoopBackOff state
fixes: Check pod logs: kubectl logs pod-name --previous
Verify the container entrypoint/command is correct
Check if liveness probe is failing and killing the container
Inspect resource limits — OOM may cause immediate restart

title: Cannot pull container image
fixes: Verify the image name and tag exist in the registry
Check imagePullSecrets is configured for private registries
Ensure the node can reach the registry (network/firewall)
Try pulling the image manually on the node: docker pull image:tag

title: Container killed due to out-of-memory
fixes: Increase memory limits in the pod spec: resources.limits.memory
Profile the application to find memory leaks
Set appropriate memory requests to ensure proper scheduling
Use JVM flags like -XX:MaxRAMPercentage for Java containers

title: Pod stuck in Pending due to insufficient resources
fixes: Scale up the cluster or add nodes with more resources
Reduce resource requests in the pod spec
Check for node taints that prevent scheduling: kubectl describe node
Remove pods with excessive resource requests from other workloads

title: Service not found or has no endpoints
fixes: Verify the service exists: kubectl get svc -n namespace
Check service selector matches pod labels exactly
Ensure pods backing the service are Running and Ready
Verify namespace — services aren't cross-namespace by default

title: ConfigMap referenced but not found
fixes: Create the ConfigMap: kubectl create configmap name --from-file=path
Check the ConfigMap name and namespace match the pod spec
Verify configMapKeyRef key exists in the ConfigMap data
Use optional: true in envFrom if ConfigMap is not always present

title: Ingress returning 404 for requests
fixes: Verify ingress rules match the request host and path
Check the backend service and port exist and have endpoints
Verify ingress controller is running: kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx
Check path type: Prefix vs Exact vs ImplementationSpecific

title: TLS certificate or cert-manager failure
fixes: Check Certificate resource status: kubectl describe certificate name
Verify cert-manager is running and ClusterIssuer is configured
Check DNS records point to the correct IP for HTTP-01 validation
Inspect cert-manager logs: kubectl logs -n cert-manager deploy/cert-manager

title: Volume mount failed - PVC not found
fixes: Create the PersistentVolumeClaim before deploying the pod
Verify PVC name in pod spec matches the actual PVC name
Check StorageClass exists and can provision volumes
Ensure PVC and Pod are in the same namespace

regex: connection refused.*:6443|Unable to connect to the server
title: Cannot connect to Kubernetes API server
fixes: Check if the cluster is running: systemctl status kubelet
Verify kubeconfig points to correct server: kubectl config view
Check if VPN is required to reach the cluster
Renew certificates if expired: kubeadm certs renew all

title: Health check probe failure
fixes: Verify the probe endpoint returns 200 when the app is healthy
Increase initialDelaySeconds if the app needs more startup time
Check the probe port matches the container's listening port
Add timeoutSeconds if the health endpoint is slow

title: RBAC permission denied
fixes: Create a RoleBinding/ClusterRoleBinding for the user/service account
Check which permissions are needed: kubectl auth can-i --list
Verify the ServiceAccount is set on the pod spec
Use kubectl auth can-i verb resource --as=user to test

title: Container repeatedly restarting
fixes: Check previous container logs: kubectl logs pod --previous
Inspect exit code: kubectl describe pod name (look for Exit Code)
Verify environment variables and secrets are set correctly
Check if the container exits immediately due to wrong CMD

title: HPA unable to fetch metrics
fixes: Verify metrics-server is running: kubectl get pods -n kube-system
Check that resource requests are set on the deployment (required for CPU HPA)
Verify custom metrics adapter is configured for custom metrics
Wait a few minutes — metrics may not be available immediately after deploy

title: Network policy blocking pod communication
fixes: Check NetworkPolicy rules: kubectl get networkpolicy -n namespace
Add an ingress/egress rule allowing traffic from the source pod's labels
Verify the CNI plugin supports NetworkPolicy (not all do)
Test connectivity: kubectl exec pod -- curl target-service:port

title: Secret referenced but not found in namespace
fixes: Create the secret: kubectl create secret generic name --from-literal=key=value
Verify secret exists in the same namespace as the pod
Check secret name in pod spec for typos
Use kubectl get secrets -n namespace to list available secrets

title: Deployment rollout stuck/timed out
fixes: Check new pods: kubectl get pods -l app=name to see why they're not Ready
Increase progressDeadlineSeconds if startup is legitimately slow
Rollback: kubectl rollout undo deployment/name
Check resource quotas aren't blocking new pod creation

regex: pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims|waiting for.*volume
title: Pod waiting for PersistentVolume to be bound
fixes: Check if a PV matching the PVC exists: kubectl get pv
Verify StorageClass provisioner is working
Check PVC accessMode and storage size match available PVs
For local volumes: ensure the node where PV is created is schedulable

title: Admission webhook rejected the request
fixes: Check the webhook error message for specific policy violations
Review the ValidatingWebhookConfiguration for the blocking webhook
Fix the resource to comply with the policy (security context, labels, etc.)
Temporarily disable the webhook if it's blocking critical fixes

title: Node at pod capacity limit
fixes: Increase max-pods in kubelet configuration
Add more nodes to the cluster
Consolidate small pods into fewer, larger containers
Check for completed/failed pods consuming slots: kubectl delete pods --field-selector=status.phase=Succeeded

regex: Error acquiring the state lock|Lock Info|lock.*already held
title: Terraform state lock cannot be acquired
fixes: Wait for the other operation to complete
Force unlock if the lock is stale: terraform force-unlock LOCK_ID
Check if a previous run crashed without releasing the lock
Verify DynamoDB table (AWS) or storage account (Azure) is accessible

regex: provider.*not found|Could not retrieve the list of available versions for provider
title: Terraform provider not found or unavailable
fixes: Run terraform init to download required providers
Add required_providers block with source in terraform {} block
Check registry connectivity (registry.terraform.io)
Use a provider mirror or filesystem_mirror for air-gapped environments

title: Resource already exists outside Terraform
fixes: Import the existing resource: terraform import resource.name id
Remove the resource from state if it shouldn't be managed: terraform state rm
Add lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true } to prevent accidental recreation
Use data source instead of resource if you just need to reference it

regex: Plan.*differ.*actual.*drift|Objects have changed outside of Terraform
title: Drift detected between state and reality
fixes: Run terraform plan to see what changed
Run terraform apply to reconcile state with config
Use terraform refresh to update state without applying
Investigate who/what made changes outside Terraform and prevent it

regex: Module not installed|Module.*not found|Could not download module
title: Terraform module not found
fixes: Run terraform init to download modules
Verify the module source path/URL is correct
Check git credentials if using private repository modules
Pin module version: source = "module?ref=v1.0.0"

title: Cloud provider credentials invalid or expired
fixes: Refresh credentials: aws sso login, az login, or gcloud auth login
Check AWS_PROFILE or environment variables are set correctly
Verify IAM role/user has required permissions
Check if temporary credentials (STS) have expired

title: Reference to undeclared Terraform object
fixes: Declare the variable in variables.tf
Check for typos in the resource reference: module.name.output
Ensure the referenced resource is in the same module or passed as variable
Run terraform validate to catch all reference errors

title: Dependency cycle in Terraform resources
fixes: Break the cycle by using depends_on with a third intermediary resource
Use data source to reference one of the resources instead
Restructure resources so dependencies flow one direction
Extract one side of the cycle into a separate module

title: Cannot destroy resource due to dependencies
fixes: Destroy dependent resources first by using target: terraform destroy -target=dependent
Remove the dependency relationship before destroying
Use force_destroy = true if the resource supports it (S3 buckets, etc.)
Empty the resource first (delete objects in bucket, detach policies)

regex: Unsupported attribute|This object does not have an attribute named
title: Accessing non-existent resource attribute
fixes: Check provider documentation for available attributes
Verify you're using the correct resource type name
Check if the attribute was renamed or removed in a provider upgrade
Use terraform console to interactively explore resource attributes

regex: value of.*cty\..*is not suitable|Inappropriate value.*string required
title: Type constraint error in variable or output
fixes: Check the variable type constraint matches the value being passed
Use tostring(), tonumber(), tolist() for explicit conversion
Verify tfvars file uses correct HCL syntax for complex types
Add explicit type in variable block: type = string

title: Backend configuration changed, init required
fixes: Run terraform init -reconfigure to reinitialize with new backend
Use terraform init -migrate-state to move state to new backend
Check that backend config values in terraform {} block are correct

title: Resource creation timed out
fixes: Increase the timeout in resource timeouts block
Check cloud provider console for the resource status
Verify network connectivity from Terraform to the cloud API
Some resources take time (RDS, EKS) — add custom timeouts { create = "60m" }

title: Invalid count or for_each argument
fixes: Ensure count value is a whole number, not a computed value that might be unknown
For for_each: use toset() to convert list to set
Cannot use for_each with values not known until apply — restructure
Move dynamic values to a local with explicit type

regex: state.*is empty|No state file found|Failed to load state
title: Terraform state file missing or empty
fixes: Check backend configuration points to correct location
Restore from state backup if available (.terraform.tfstate.backup)
Import existing resources: terraform import resource.name id
If migrating backends, use terraform init -migrate-state

title: Provider inconsistency between plan and apply
fixes: Upgrade the provider to latest version
Add ignore_changes lifecycle for the drifting attribute
Report as provider bug if consistently reproducible
Run apply again — may resolve on second attempt

title: Insufficient IAM permissions for Terraform operation
fixes: Add the required IAM policy to the Terraform execution role
Check the error for the specific action and resource ARN needed
Use AWS IAM Access Analyzer to generate minimum-privilege policy
Verify you're using the correct AWS account/profile

title: Duplicate resource definition in configuration
fixes: Remove the duplicate resource block
Use count or for_each for multiple similar resources
If imported twice, remove from state: terraform state rm one
Rename one resource and use moved block for state migration

title: Saved plan is stale due to state changes
fixes: Regenerate the plan: terraform plan -out=newplan
Ensure no other process is modifying state concurrently
Apply immediately after plan to reduce staleness window
Use -lock=true to prevent concurrent modifications

title: Provider plugin version incompatible or missing
fixes: Run terraform init -upgrade to download compatible provider version
Pin provider version in required_providers: version = "~> 4.0"
Delete .terraform directory and re-run terraform init
Check if the provider was renamed (e.g., hashicorp/aws vs aws)
